mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term predictive IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, positive month-over-month growth in the industrial production index signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher financing costs, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative forward returns in more than 55% of positive industrial production growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where the consumer price index exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is subsumed by single-series predictors.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where real GDP shows positive growth, or the predictive IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial production below its recent trend signals weakening manufacturing demand and supply chain contraction, which triggers negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of instances where the industrial production index crosses below its 6-month average.
